audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Isaac famine/sojourning, promise repetition, Rebekah sister-wife episode, wells/conflict, Rehoboth, Beersheba oath, and Esau's Hittite marriages.

verses.restored: There was a famine in the land, besides the first famine that was in the days of Abraham. Isaac went to Abimelech king of the Philistines, to Gerar.

familiar.text: And there was a famine in the land, beside the first famine...

notes.title: Isaac repeats Abraham patterns
notes.body: Famine, Gerar, Abimelech, and wife-as-sister motifs echo Abraham’s story.

verses.restored: The Eternal appeared to him and said, Do not go down to Egypt. Dwell in the land that I tell you.

familiar.text: And the LORD appeared unto him, and said, Go not down into Egypt...

verses.restored: Sojourn in this land, and I will be with you and bless you, for to you and to your seed I will give all these lands, and I will establish the oath that I swore to Abraham your father.

familiar.text: Sojourn in this land, and I will be with thee, and will bless thee...

notes.title: Promise renewed to Isaac
notes.body: The Abrahamic promise is explicitly transferred to Isaac: presence, blessing, seed, land, and oath.

verses.restored: I will multiply your seed like the stars of the heavens, and I will give to your seed all these lands. In your seed all the nations of the earth shall be blessed,

familiar.text: And I will make thy seed to multiply as the stars of heaven...

verses.restored: because Abraham listened to my voice and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my instructions.

familiar.text: Because that Abraham obeyed my voice, and kept my charge...

verses.restored: So Isaac dwelled in Gerar.

familiar.text: And Isaac dwelt in Gerar:

verses.restored: The men of the place asked about his woman. He said, She is my sister, for he feared to say, My woman, lest the men of the place kill me because of Rebekah, for she was good in appearance.

familiar.text: And the men of the place asked him of his wife; and he said, She is my sister...

notes.title: Sister-wife pattern again
notes.body: Isaac repeats the fear-driven deception seen with Abraham and Sarah.

verses.restored: When he had been there many days, Abimelech king of the Philistines looked out through the window and saw, and look, Isaac was laughing/caressing Rebekah his woman.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, when he had been there a long time... Isaac was sporting with Rebekah...

notes.title: Laughing/caressing
notes.body: Metsacheq is related to Isaac’s laughter-name and here shows marital intimacy, exposing the deception.

verses.restored: Abimelech called Isaac and said, Surely she is your woman. How then did you say, She is my sister? Isaac said to him, Because I said, Lest I die because of her.

familiar.text: And Abimelech called Isaac, and said, Behold, of a surety she is thy wife...

verses.restored: Abimelech said, What is this you have done to us? One of the people might easily have lain with your woman, and you would have brought guilt upon us.

familiar.text: And Abimelech said, What is this thou hast done unto us?...

notes.title: Guilt avoided
notes.body: As in Genesis 20, the foreign king recognizes the danger of covenant household deception.

verses.restored: Abimelech commanded all the people, saying, Whoever touches this man or his woman shall surely be put to death.

familiar.text: And Abimelech charged all his people, saying, He that toucheth this man...

verses.restored: Isaac sowed in that land and found in that year a hundredfold, and the Eternal blessed him.

familiar.text: Then Isaac sowed in that land, and received in the same year an hundredfold...

verses.restored: The man became great, and he kept going and becoming great until he became very great.

familiar.text: And the man waxed great, and went forward, and grew until he became very great:

verses.restored: He had possessions of flocks and possessions of herds and many servants, and the Philistines envied him.

familiar.text: For he had possession of flocks, and possession of herds...

verses.restored: All the wells that his father’s servants had dug in the days of Abraham his father, the Philistines stopped up and filled with dust.

familiar.text: For all the wells which his father's servants had digged...

verses.restored: Abimelech said to Isaac, Go away from us, for you are much mightier than we are.

familiar.text: And Abimelech said unto Isaac, Go from us; for thou art much mightier than we.

verses.restored: Isaac went from there, encamped in the valley of Gerar, and dwelled there.

familiar.text: And Isaac departed thence, and pitched his tent in the valley of Gerar...

verses.restored: Isaac dug again the wells of water that had been dug in the days of Abraham his father, which the Philistines had stopped up after Abraham’s death, and he called their names by the names his father had called them.

familiar.text: And Isaac digged again the wells of water...

notes.title: Re-digging wells
notes.body: Isaac restores Abraham’s wells and names, re-opening ancestral life sources.

verses.restored: Isaac’s servants dug in the valley and found there a well of living water.

familiar.text: And Isaac's servants digged in the valley, and found there a well of springing water.

notes.title: Living water
notes.body: Mayim chayyim means living/running water, not stagnant water.

verses.restored: The herders of Gerar quarreled with Isaac’s herders, saying, The water is ours. He called the name of the well Esek, because they contended with him.

familiar.text: And the herdmen of Gerar did strive with Isaac's herdmen...

verses.restored: They dug another well, and they quarreled over that also, so he called its name Sitnah.

familiar.text: And they digged another well, and strove for that also...

verses.restored: He moved from there and dug another well, and they did not quarrel over it. He called its name Rehoboth and said, For now the Eternal has made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land.

familiar.text: And he removed from thence, and digged another well...

notes.title: Rehoboth
notes.body: Rehoboth comes from room/broad space. Conflict gives way to spaciousness.

verses.restored: From there he went up to Beersheba.

familiar.text: And he went up from thence to Beersheba.

verses.restored: The Eternal appeared to him that night and said, I am the Divine of Abraham your father. Do not fear, for I am with you. I will bless you and multiply your seed for the sake of Abraham my servant.

familiar.text: And the LORD appeared unto him the same night...

verses.restored: He built an altar there and called on the name of the Eternal. He pitched his tent there, and Isaac’s servants dug a well there.

familiar.text: And he builded an altar there, and called upon the name of the LORD...

verses.restored: Abimelech came to him from Gerar, with Ahuzzath his companion and Phicol commander of his army.

familiar.text: Then Abimelech went to him from Gerar, and Ahuzzath one of his friends...

verses.restored: Isaac said to them, Why have you come to me, since you hated me and sent me away from you?

familiar.text: And Isaac said unto them, Wherefore come ye to me, seeing ye hate me...

verses.restored: They said, We have clearly seen that the Eternal has been with you, and we said, Let there now be an oath between us, between us and you, and let us cut a covenant with you,

familiar.text: And they said, We saw certainly that the LORD was with thee...

verses.restored: that you will do us no harm, just as we have not touched you, and just as we have done to you only good and sent you away in peace. You are now blessed of the Eternal.

familiar.text: That thou wilt do us no hurt, as we have not touched thee...

verses.restored: He made them a feast, and they ate and drank.

familiar.text: And he made them a feast, and they did eat and drink.

verses.restored: They rose early in the morning and swore to one another. Isaac sent them away, and they went from him in peace.

familiar.text: And they rose up betimes in the morning, and sware one to another...

verses.restored: That same day Isaac’s servants came and told him about the well that they had dug, and said to him, We have found water.

familiar.text: And it came to pass the same day, that Isaac's servants came...

verses.restored: He called it Shibah. Therefore the name of the city is Beersheba to this day.

familiar.text: And he called it Shebah: therefore the name of the city is Beersheba...

notes.title: Shibah / Beersheba
notes.body: The name plays again on oath/seven and well.

verses.restored: When Esau was forty years old, he took Judith daughter of Beeri the Hittite and Basemath daughter of Elon the Hittite as women.

familiar.text: And Esau was forty years old when he took to wife Judith...

verses.restored: They were bitterness of spirit to Isaac and to Rebekah.

familiar.text: Which were a grief of mind unto Isaac and to Rebekah.

notes.title: Bitterness of spirit
notes.body: The phrase names deep distress caused by Esau’s marriages.
